data.title: F5 Shareholders Sue Company Over Security Breach Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against F5 (FFIV) on December 19, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Plaintiffs in the federal securities class action allege that they acquired F5 stock at artificially inflated prices between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, known as the "Class Period." They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of F5's alleged misconduct during that time.

Investors who bought F5 stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

F5 is a global multicloud application security and delivery company. It provides various distributed cloud services; application security and delivery products; and application management solutions. It also makes assorted professional services, such as maintenance, consulting, training, and extra technical support services available to its customers in both the public and private sectors.

Based in Seattle, F5 was founded in 1996 and now has approximately 6,500 employees. It used to be known as F5 Networks, Inc., and adopted its current moniker back in 2021.

Summary of Facts

The company and four of its senior officers (the "Individual Defendants") are now accused of deceiving investors by lying and withholding important information about F5's business and prospects during the Class Period.

In particular, they are accused of omitting truthful information about a security breach affecting the company from SEC filings and related material. By knowingly or recklessly doing so, they allegedly caused F5 stock to trade at artificially inflated prices during the time in question.

After a series of partial disclosures, the truth fully emerged after the market closed on October 27, 2025. That's when F5 announced its fourth quarter fiscal year 2025 results. In this context, the company disclosed "significantly below-market growth expectations for fiscal 2026," which it mostly blamed on  the Security Breach affecting F5. The company also announced "expected reductions to sales and renewals, elongated sales cycles, terminated projections, and increased expenses attributed to ongoing remediation efforts."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Gains Despite Growing Concerns About ICE Connection
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has been in the hot seat of late over what looks like connections to Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), and by extension, to the Trump administration. Investors are growing increasingly concerned about these connections, noted a new report from Reuters. But investors kept their skepticism limited, as evidenced by the nearly 1.5% gain in Home Depot stock seen in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report noted that investors—backed up by a group with sustainability on its mind—are calling for a review of Home Depot's connection with Flock Safety. Flock Safety handles corporate security measures, including gathering license plate data. The report suggested that local law enforcement agencies were taking the license plate data Flock Safety generated, and passing that information on to ICE.

Most of this seems above board, so about the only place the investor coalition can object is at Flock Safety. The investor coalition wants an "…assessment of privacy and civil rights risks, including discrimination or wrongful detention from misuse of customer data." Such misuse may, in turn, "…expose the Company to financial and legal risks, including potential data breaches and enforcement of evolving state privacy laws."

Backing Up Disaster Recovery

Meanwhile, Home Depot Foundation is taking a step forward in trying to narrow the gap between skilled labor and disaster recovery, which is suffering from a shortage of exactly that. Thus, the Foundation is starting up new programs that "…focus on training and credentialing veterans and other workers for disaster recovery roles."

This notion became particularly compelling after the California wildfires, reports noted, when a clear call emerged for disaster recovery professionals to help rebuild communities. The Foundation's work with veterans, meanwhile, may help provide a ready and partially-trained labor pool for such efforts.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys, five Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 8.53% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $407.67 per share implies 7.11% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/21/2026?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday after President Trump ruled out using force to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I don't have to use force. I don't want to use force. I won't use force. All the United States is asking for is a place called Greenland," Trump said at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.

Trump Eyes Greenland Negotiations, Sees Ukraine-Russia Deal as Close

While the statement helped ease tensions between the U.S., Greenland, and the European Union, Trump added that he would seek immediate negotiations to begin the process of acquiring Greenland and highlighted the territory's importance to national security.

Meanwhile, Trump noted that a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia was reasonably close ahead of meetings with the presidents of both countries later this week. "If they don't get this done, they are stupid," he said.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Is a July IPO for SpaceX Elon Musk's Answer to OpenAI and Anthropic?
data.description:
News of a possible initial public offering (IPO) this year from space company SpaceX and heavyweight AI startups OpenAI and Anthropic has been flying around for a while now. However, the Wall Street Journal in a new report has said SpaceX CEO, billionaire Elon Musk, is targeting to take the company public by July to beat the other two to the market.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk is said to view taking SpaceX public as a way to give his xAI startup a boost so it can narrow the gap with the rival AI firms, according to the report. Last week, the New York Times reported that SpaceX has interviewed banks to lead the offering. Now, the Journal is saying the chief executive could make the decision soon.

Startups Eye IPO as AI Race Intensifies

Earlier reports indicated that SpaceX — which was most recently valued at $800 billion — could follow a faster route for its public debut through a merger with EchoStar (SATS), the wireless phone and satellite TV services provider. EchoStar in November upped its stake in SpaceX to about 3% after it sold new spectrum licenses worth about $2.6 billion to the space firm.

The update comes as OpenAI, which is backed by U.S. tech giant Microsoft (MSFT), this week disclosed that its annual recurring revenue jumped tenfold from $2 billion in 2023 to over $20 billion in 2025, with the demand for its computing capacity rising by about the same percentage. This is even as data has shown that Alphabet-owned (GOOGL) Google's Gemini chatbot is closing in on OpenAI's ChatGPT.

While OpenAI is reportedly seeking up to $100 billion in new funding to lift its valuation to about $750 billion, Anthropic is pursuing roughly $10 billion to reach a market value near $350 billion.

What Are the Best AI Stocks to Buy?

There are several AI players and stocks on Wall Street. The TipRanks Stock Comparison tool, based on the latest ratings by analysts, highlights which of them are currently considered a good investment opportunity. Kindly refer to the image below.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Trump's Credit Card Rate Cap Would be an 'Economist Disaster,' Warns Jamie Dimon
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon cautioned that President Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates would lead to an "economic disaster."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"In the worst case, you'd have to have a drastic reduction of the credit card business," Dimon said during a panel at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland. "I mean drastic, I mean like 10%, I mean like 80%. You would remove credit from 80% of Americans and that is their backup credit."

Dimon Says U.S. Should Test Capping Credit Card Interest Rates

At the same time, Dimon suggested, perhaps jokingly, that the U.S. should test the cap in two states, Vermont and Massachusetts. Senators Bernie Sanders of Vermont and Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts have both backed a bill that would cap credit card interest rates at 10% for five years, although Dimon didn't explicitly mention them.

Dimon added that retailers, restaurants, travel companies, and schools would be the biggest losers of the cap, not credit card companies.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: GameStop (GME) Is Expected to Close 470 Stores by End of January
data.description:
Video game retailer GameStop (GME) is expected to close a large number of stores this month across several major U.S. states, including California, Texas, New York, Florida, and Pennsylvania. More specifically, an online tracker that compiles store shutdowns shows that at least 470 locations will be affected by the end of January. Notably, California is expected to see about 50 closures, followed by 44 in Texas, 30 in New York, 25 in Florida, and 24 in Pennsylvania.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

When these January closures are added in, the total number of GameStop stores that have shut down over the past two years is estimated to reach at least 1,000. Interestingly, during the company's peak years, it operated more than 6,000 stores across the U.S. However, once the latest round of shutdowns ends, GameStop is expected to be left with fewer than 2,000 locations, which highlights just how much its physical retail footprint has shrunk.

Nevertheless, despite the store closures, investor sentiment improved today after CEO Ryan Cohen increased his personal stake in GameStop. In fact, shares rose after Cohen bought an additional 500,000 shares at an average price of $21.12, according to an SEC filing. That purchase increases his ownership to roughly 9.2%, or more than 41.5 million shares when including warrants, for a total of about $117 million. Cohen, who first took a major stake in 2020 and became CEO in 2023, has also been granted large performance-based stock options tied to aggressive long-term targets.

Is GameStop a Good Stock to Buy Now?

According to TipRanks A.I. Analyst, GameStop has a Neutral rating of 62 and a price target of $22, implying 1.71% upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Teledyne Technologies Stock (TDY) Jumps 10% on Strong Financial Results
data.description:
Shares of Teledyne Technologies (TDY) are up 10% and leading the benchmark S&P 500 index higher after it reported better-than-expected quarterly financial results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Teledyne Technologies, which primarily makes electronic instruments used for digital imaging, satellites, and defense systems, reported earnings per share of $6.30 for the fourth quarter of 2025. That was far ahead of the $5.83 a share forecast on Wall Street. Revenue totaled $1.6 billion, ahead of analysts' consensus expectation of $1.57 billion. Sales were up 7% from a year earlier.

TDY stock is up 10% and on track for its biggest single-day gain since March 2020 when the Covid-19 pandemic struck. Teledyne Technologies is also the top performing stock in the S&P 500 index on Jan. 21 following news of a milestone quarter for the company.

Teledyne's Defense Business

In addition to the top and bottom line beats, TDY stock is also getting a lift after the company reported record quarterly orders, sales, and operating margins. Investors also appear to like that Teledyne reported sales in its defense and aerospace segment rose 40% year-over-year in Q4 2025.

"Our defense businesses remained healthy, and our shorter cycle commercial businesses continued to recover with most product families increasing either sequentially or year-over-year," said Teledyne Executive Chairman Robert Mehrabian in the company's earnings release. Teledyne guided for earnings of $23.45 to $23.85 in 2026, inline with Wall Street's expectations. So far in 2026, TDY stock has risen 22%.

Is TDY Stock a Buy?

Teledyne Technologies' stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among seven Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TDY price target of $614.71 implies 1.14% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: MTY Food Group (MTY) Raises Dividend 12%
data.description:
Canadian restaurant operator MTY Food Group (TSE:MTY) has raised its quarterly dividend by 12%.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Montreal-based company says it will now pay a quarterly dividend of C$0.37 a share, up from C$0.33 previously. The increase pushes the dividend yield on MTY stock to 3.7%. The new dividend is payable on Feb. 13 to shareholders of record as of Feb. 3.

MTY Food Group operates more than 80 restaurant brands through a franchise model. It mostly runs restaurants found in food courts at shopping malls. Banners include Thai Express, Manchu Wok, and Pizza Delight. It also owns high-end restaurants such as Baton Rouge and Van Houtte coffee.

MTY Food Group's Cash Flow

In a statement, MTY Food Group CEO Eric Lefebvre said the increased dividend underscores the company's confidence in its ability to generate sustainable cash flow and deliver long-term value to shareholders.

The dividend hike also comes after MTY Food Group announced late last year that it has initiated a review of strategic alternatives, including the possible sale of all or part of its business. A potential sale is being considered with MTY stock down 20% in the last 12 months.

Is MTY Stock a Buy?

The stock of MTY Food Group has a consensus Hold rating among two Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MTY price target of C$42.36 implies 3.28% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: YouTube Targets 'AI Slop' and Deepfakes as Top Priorities for 2026
data.description:
Neal Mohan, the CEO of video-sharing platform YouTube, said that cutting down on low-quality "AI slop" and improving deepfake detection will be major priorities in 2026. In his annual letter, Mohan warned that it is becoming increasingly difficult to tell the difference between real and AI-generated content, especially when it comes to deepfake videos. Furthermore, as artificial intelligence becomes more widespread, YouTube's parent company, tech giant Google (GOOGL), continues to invest heavily in AI infrastructure and models, while YouTube faces a surge in AI-created video uploads.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unfortunately, this surge includes a flood of low-quality AI videos that are designed to game recommendation algorithms. As a result, Mohan said that YouTube is responding by improving its spam and clickbait-fighting systems, while clearly labeling AI-generated content and requiring creators to disclose when videos have been altered. The platform also removes harmful synthetic media that violates its rules and is building tools that detect when a creator's likeness is used without permission.

At the same time, YouTube wants to continue supporting creators rather than replacing them with AI. Indeed, Mohan said that more than one million channels used YouTube's AI creation tools each day in December, and the company plans to expand these features across formats like Shorts. Creators will soon also be able to generate content using their own likeness, create games from text prompts, and experiment with music. In addition, YouTube is focused on helping creators earn more through shopping, brand deals, and fan-funding tools, while making the platform safer and easier to use.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 27 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $343.65 per share implies 3.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
